title: Event-Log: JSONL Writer/Reader + Env-Var Contract
summary: Append-only event log as the single internal truth. Package internal/eventlog provides an Event struct, an append-only Writer, a Reader, env-var context resolution (GOLEMIC_RUN_ID, GOLEMIC_EVENT_LOG), and event-type constants with payload validation for review_submitted.

stakeholder_intent.goal: Write and read structured events to/from an append-only JSONL file per run
stakeholder_intent.business_value: Single authoritative event channel: the runner, dev, and reviewer all communicate through the same append-only log, enabling deterministic outcome determination, audit, and future recovery
stakeholder_intent.trigger: Runner starts a run (creates event log), agent subcommands (emit, open-pr, submit-review) append events, runner reads events after a role finishes
stakeholder_intent.success_outcome: Every event is durably appended; the reader can reconstruct the full ordered event sequence; malformed lines fail closed with a clear error

in_scope: Event struct with type, ts (RFC3339), runId, payload (JSON object)
Append-only Writer: creates ~/.golemic/<project>/runs/<runId>/events.jsonl (creating directories), one line per event, O_APPEND
Reader: reads entire file, malformed lines → error (fail-closed, no skip), helper LastEventOfType(type)
Env-var context resolution: GOLEMIC_RUN_ID and GOLEMIC_EVENT_LOG read from environment; missing either → clear error, exit != 0
Event-type constants for all events in §2.6: run_started, worktree_created, dev_started, pr_opened, review_submitted, run_finished
Payload validation for review_submitted: verdict must be approved or changes_requested
out_of_scope: Recovery logic (reconstructing run state from log)

preconditions: Config (I0.2) and credentials (I0.3) are available to determine ~/.golemic/<project>/ path
Run ID is generated before the event log is created

business_rules.rule: The event log is append-only; only the Writer appends, never overwrites or truncates
business_rules.outcome: O_APPEND flag is used; existing data is never modified

business_rules.rule: Malformed JSONL lines in the event log are a hard error; they must not be skipped
business_rules.applies_when: Reader encounters a line that is not valid JSON matching the Event struct
business_rules.outcome: Reader returns an error naming the line number and the parse failure; the caller must not proceed

business_rules.rule: Env vars GOLEMIC_RUN_ID and GOLEMIC_EVENT_LOG are mandatory for all subcommands that write to the event log
business_rules.outcome: Missing var → clear error message listing the missing var, exit != 0, no event written

business_rules.rule: The review_submitted payload must contain a verdict field with value approved or changes_requested
business_rules.applies_when: A review_submitted event is written or validated
business_rules.outcome: Invalid verdict → error, event not written

business_rules.rule: Concurrent appends from multiple writers must not lose lines
business_rules.applies_when: Two writer processes append to the same file concurrently
business_rules.outcome: Each write is a single O_APPEND write(2) call; lines may interleave but each line is complete

decision_tables.name: Event log path resolution
decision_tables.default_outcome: Error: insufficient context to determine event log path

inputs.name: event
constraints: type must be a known constant or non-empty string
runId must match the current run
payload must be a valid JSON object

outputs.condition: Event is valid and write succeeds

errors.condition: Event log directory cannot be created
errors.message: Failed to create event log directory: <path>
Failed to open event log file: <path>

inputs.name: path
constraints: Must be an absolute path to an existing events.jsonl file

outputs.body: Slice of Event structs in file order

outputs.condition: At least one event of the given type exists
outputs.body: Pointer to the last (most recent) Event of that type

outputs.condition: No event of the given type exists

errors.condition: No event of the requested type found
errors.message: No event of type <eventType> found in log

integration_contracts.operation: Append one JSONL line to events.jsonl
integration_contracts.request_contract: One JSON object serialized to a single line, terminated by \n
integration_contracts.idempotency: No built-in idempotency; caller ensures deduplication via runId and event ordering
integration_contracts.timeout: No timeout; file system write is expected to complete quickly
integration_contracts.retry_policy: No retry; write failure is fatal for the current operation
integration_contracts.failure_mapping: File system error → LOG_FILE_OPEN_FAILED or LOG_DIR_CREATE_FAILED
integration_contracts.compatibility: JSONL format is stable; new fields in Event struct are additive

integration_contracts.operation: Read GOLEMIC_RUN_ID and GOLEMIC_EVENT_LOG from environment
integration_contracts.request_contract: Strings set by the runner before spawning agent subprocesses
integration_contracts.response_contract: Empty string if var is not set
integration_contracts.idempotency: Environment is read-only for the subprocess; idempotent by nature
integration_contracts.retry_policy: No retry; missing env var is a hard error
integration_contracts.failure_mapping: Missing var → clear error message listing the missing var name
integration_contracts.compatibility: Env var names are stable; new vars are additive

state_changes.precondition: File exists (or directory is created) and opened with O_APPEND

changes.value_source: Serialized JSON object of the Event struct

authorization_rules: Event log resides under ~/.golemic/<project>/ which is machine-local and not in the repo
audit_requirements: Every event is recorded in the append-only log with timestamp

architecture_constraints: Writer must be safe for concurrent use (single write(2) call per line)
Reader must fail closed on malformed lines
No external dependencies beyond Go standard library
allowed_changes: Add new fields to Event struct (backward-compatible)
forbidden_changes: Do not skip malformed lines in the reader
Do not change the JSONL line format (one JSON object per line)
dependencies: Standard library: encoding/json, os, time, fmt, path/filepath
implementation_context.consistency_and_concurrency: Each write is a single O_APPEND write(2) call; the OS kernel serializes concurrent appends. No Go-level mutex is needed at the file level but the Writer should be goroutine-safe via a mutex if shared.

acceptance_scenarios.title: Round-trip: write then read returns identical events
given: A temp directory for the event log
A valid Event struct with known type, runId, and payload
when: The event is written via Writer.Write
The file is read via Reader.Read
then: The returned slice contains exactly one Event
All fields match the original Event (type, ts, runId, payload)

acceptance_scenarios.title: Malformed line in log file causes error
given: An events.jsonl file exists with one valid line and one invalid line
when: Reader.Read is called on the file
then: An error is returned indicating the malformed line number

acceptance_scenarios.title: Concurrent appends maintain line integrity
given: Two Writer instances writing to the same file concurrently
then: Each write is a single O_APPEND write(2) call
No line is truncated or interleaved with another

acceptance_scenarios.title: Event log path resolution uses env vars or constructs path
given: GOLEMIC_EVENT_LOG is set to an absolute path
when: The context resolution function is called

acceptance_scenarios.title: Directory is created on first write
given: The event log directory does not exist
when: Writer.Write is called with a valid event
then: The directory and events.jsonl file are created

acceptance_scenarios.title: Concurrent appends lose no lines
given: Two Writer instances writing to the same events.jsonl file
then: All events are parseable and valid

acceptance_scenarios.title: Missing env var returns clear error
then: A named error is returned listing both missing variables

acceptance_scenarios.title: LastEventOfType returns the most recent event
given: An event log with three events: run_started, dev_started, run_started
then: The returned event is the second run_started (index 2)

test_cases: Concurrent appends from two goroutines produce no data loss
LastEventOfType on empty list returns error
Payload validation for review_submitted accepts approved and changes_requested
Payload validation for review_submitted rejects invalid verdict
Event log directory is created on first write
non_functional_requirements: Writer must be safe for concurrent use
No external dependencies beyond Go standard library
definition_of_done: go build ./... && go vet ./... && go test -count=1 ./internal/eventlog/... is green
All event type constants are defined
Payload validation for review_submitted is implemented

decision_log.decision: Use JSONL (one JSON object per line) for the append-only event log
decision_log.rationale: Specified in §2.6: append-only JSONL, one line per event

decision_log.topic: Env var contract for context resolution
decision_log.decision: Subcommands resolve their run context via GOLEMIC_RUN_ID and GOLEMIC_EVENT_LOG environment variables
decision_log.rationale: Specified in §2.6: the runner sets these when spawning the agent subprocess

decision_log.decision: The reader must not skip malformed lines; it must return an error
decision_log.rationale: Specified in the backlog: 'malformed Zeilen → Fehler (fail-closed, kein Überspringen)'

decision_log.decision: review_submitted event must validate verdict ∈ {approved, changes_requested}
decision_log.rationale: Specified in the backlog: 'Payload-Validierung für review_submitted (verdict ∈ {approved,changes_requested})'

decision_log.decision: Event log context resolution is available for any subcommand that needs it
decision_log.rationale: cmd/golemic/main.go dispatches subcommands by name; new subcommands (emit, open-pr, submit-review) will use the same dispatch

decision_log.decision: Event log path construction uses the project name from config (I0.2)
decision_log.rationale: internal/config provides the project name from .golemic/config.json

decision_log.topic: Credentials loader for project name validation
decision_log.decision: Project name validation in credentials.Loader is reused for event log path construction
decision_log.rationale: internal/credentials validates project names to prevent path traversal; same validation applies to event log path

decision_log.decision: Event log uses direct os.File operations, not the Executor interface (file I/O, not external commands)
decision_log.rationale: The Executor interface is designed for external commands (gh, git, pi); event log uses standard library file I/O directly

decision_log.decision: Event log package uses only Go standard library, consistent with the module's zero-dependency policy
decision_log.rationale: go.mod has no external dependencies; event log must not introduce any

codebase_evidence.finding: Subcommand dispatch exists with preflight, run, emit, open-pr, submit-review; all except preflight are stubbed with 'not implemented'
Config struct with Project, VerifyCommand, Label, Models, TimeoutMinutes fields exists; Load function reads .golemic/config.json
Credentials loader with injectable homeDir exists; env var precedence over file is implemented
Executor interface with Run and RunWithEnv methods exists for injecting external command execution
Module is 'golemic', Go 1.21, no external dependencies
